request.question: The band that released the 2001 album "Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia" was formed in what city and state?

title: Pneumonia (album)
Deeper the Wound
Get Off (The Dandy Warhols song)
Blue Gardenia (album)
Thirteen Tales of Love and Revenge
The Dandy Warhols
Bohemian Like You
Driving to Damascus
Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia
Godless (song)
sentences: Pneumonia is the third and final studio album by the alternative country band Whiskeytown, released on May 22, 2001 on Lost Highway Records.

The album is noted for its troubled history which saw the band lose its record deal in the midst of the merger between Polygram and Universal Music Group, and the already volatile band fell apart as a result.
The album sat on the shelf for nearly two years and it was said that over 100 songs were recorded during the 3 years.
It was bootlegged heavily and gained a reputation as a great "lost" record from fans, before getting released by Lost Highway Records as something of an appetizer for Ryan Adams' 2001 album "Gold".
Deeper the Wound is a split album between American band Converge and Japanese band Hellchild.
The album was released through Deathwish Inc. in America on April 23, 2001.
"Deeper the Wound" was also the first album released through Deathwish.
It was later released by Bastardize Records in Japan in 2006.
The album features one new track from each artist, one cover song from each artist, and a few live versions of previously released tracks.
The new track "Thaw" was later released on Converge's 2001 album, "Jane Doe".
"Get Off" is a song by American rock band The Dandy Warhols.
It was released in 2000 as the first single from their third studio album, "Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia", and was re-released in 2002.
Blue Gardenia is a 2001 album by Etta James, released through the record label Private Music.
It was produced by John Snyder, who had worked with James on five of her previous studio albums.
"Blue Gardenia" contains thirteen jazz standards from the 1930s, 1940s and 1950s.
All of the standards were arranged by pianist Cedar Walton, with the exception of "Love Letters", which was arranged by Josh Sklair.
Between November 2000 and February 2001, Snyder and Walton assembled musicians to record tracks while James was recovering from a flu; her vocals were added following her recovery.
In addition to Walton, artists appearing on the album included Red Holloway on tenor saxophone and Dorothy Hawkins, James' mother, who provided vocals on the title track.
Hawkins died in May 2002, less than a year after the album's release.
Thirteen Tales of Love and Revenge is the third studio album by American duo The Pierces, released on March 20, 2007 by Lizard King Records.
The Dandy Warhols are an American alternative rock band, formed in Portland, Oregon in 1994 by singer-guitarist Courtney Taylor-Taylor and guitarist Peter Holmström.
They were joined by keyboardist Zia McCabe and drummer Eric Hedford.
Hedford left in 1998 and was replaced by Taylor-Taylor's cousin Brent DeBoer.
The band's name is a play on the name of American pop artist Andy Warhol.
"Bohemian Like You" is a song by American alternative rock band The Dandy Warhols.
The song was written by frontman Courtney Taylor-Taylor after seeing a woman pull up in her car to the traffic lights outside his apartment.
It was released as the second single from the band's third studio album, "Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia", in August 2000.
Driving to Damascus is the eighth studio album by Scottish rock band Big Country.
It was released in 1999 as both a standard edition and a limited edition digipack, and with bonus tracks in 2002.
In the U.S. it was released under a different name, "John Wayne's Dream".

The limited edition version featured different cover artwork, and included two tracks by Stuart Adamson's alt-country side project, The Raphaels ("Shattered Cross" and "Too Many Ghosts", subsequently released on the 2001 album "Supernatural"), although there was no indication in the credits that these were not by Big Country.

"Driving to Damascus" marks the band's last studio album to feature vocalist Stuart Adamson (who would die in 2001) and bassist Tony Butler (who retired from the band in 2012), and the last studio album until "The Journey" was released in 2013 with The Alarm vocalist Mike Peters taking over for Adamson and Simple Minds bassists Derek Forbes replacing Butler.
Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia is the third studio album by American rock band The Dandy Warhols.
It was released on August 1, 2000, through record label Capitol.
"Godless" is a song by American rock band The Dandy Warhols.
It is the third single from their third studio album, "Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia", released on 17 July 2001.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include both entity tokens from the inputs and a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the evidence bridge to retrieve (e.g., "EntityA EntityB director").
- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single text field only):
- Output exactly one concise summary string (no extra fields, labels, or metadata). Keep the summary to 1–2 short sentences unless the evidence forces a single-token identification (see below).
- Primary goal: integrate the first-hop identity in `context` with facts found in `passages` so the final answer agent has the explicit, supported linkage it needs.

When the question requires linking the context entity to information in the passages and the passages supply the bridging fact:
- Produce a concise (1–2 sentence) integrated summary that (1) names the context entity (using the form present in `context`), (2) names the supporting entity/attribute found in `passages` (using the form present in the inputs), and (3) states the explicit relation or fact (membership, creator attribution, partnership, founding/establishment, award, etc.) exactly as supported by the inputs. Use only facts present in `context` and `passages` and avoid introducing any unsupported details.

Name and attribution formatting:
- If a canonical multi-token name (given + family name or full official name) appears in the inputs, use that full form in the summary; do not truncate to a surname or partial name.
- For questions about creator/author/credit, prefer the person-name attribution as given in the inputs over returning the title of a work or event.

Identification requests:
- If the question explicitly asks for a single identification (for example, asks "which X" or otherwise requests a named token or label), return exactly the canonical name(s) as presented in the passages. If multiple valid names are requested, return them comma-separated. Do not add explanatory clauses, relational fragments, or extra text in these cases—only the canonical name(s).

When evidence is missing or ambiguous:
- If the passages do not supply a clear bridging fact, state that succinctly (e.g., "No supporting evidence in the provided passages" or "Unclear from the provided sources"). Retain uncertainty rather than asserting unsupported facts.

Conciseness and fidelity constraints:
- Keep the summary short and factual (1–2 sentences). Do not expand into long biographies, exhaustive lists, or unrelated details.
- Do not assert facts that are not present in `context` and `passages`.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passage text, but short verbatim names or phrases necessary for fidelity are allowed.
- Treat the `context` field as the primary first-hop identity to be linked to `passages` when integrating evidence.

Produce the `summary` now based only on the supplied `question`, `context`, and `passages`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use summary_1 and summary_2 as the sole evidential basis for the final answer; give greatest weight to summary_2 (the later, post-retrieval integrated evidence). Do not introduce facts from outside these summaries.

Output rules (apply in order):

1) If summary_1 and summary_2 clearly and unambiguously agree on the same concise answer, set the value of `answer` to exactly one minimal string and nothing else:
   - For a binary yes/no conclusion, emit the single lowercase token: yes or no.
   - For any other concise label, emit that label exactly as it appears in the summaries (trim leading/trailing whitespace and strip any surrounding punctuation or wrapper syntax such as surrounding parentheses, quotes, or JSON-like braces). Do not add words, sentences, punctuation, quotes, brackets, or any other surrounding text.

2) Preserve multi-token labels: if any visible summary contains a multi-token form of the answer (for example a full personal name, a multi-word company or place), output that full multi-token form verbatim. Only shorten a label if no multi-token form appears in either summary.

3) Company/developer preference: when the question requests a company or developer and the summaries show a subordinate studio together with an explicit parent/publisher label (e.g., in parentheses or with phrasing like "published by"), choose the parent/publisher name as the single minimal answer string. If no parent/publisher is explicitly indicated, choose the best-supported developer/studio name from the summaries.

4) If the summaries conflict or are ambiguous, do NOT apply the unanimous-agreement rule in (1). Instead, use summary_2 as the primary basis and emit the minimal concise string supported by summary_2, applying rules (2) and (3) as relevant. If summary_2 provides no concise supported answer, set `answer` to the minimal explicit token "unknown".

Formatting constraints (must always be followed):
- Produce exactly one field: `answer`.
- The value of `answer` must be a single minimal string (no surrounding text, punctuation, explanation, or structural wrappers).
- Do not output extra fields, labels, or markup.

Additional guidance:
- If summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge but summary_2 provides a resolved, supported conclusion, use the conclusion from summary_2.
- If a concise answer string can be drawn verbatim from the summaries, prefer that verbatim text over paraphrase.

End.

response.hop1_query: The band that released the 2001 album "Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia" was formed in what city and state?
hop1_docs: Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia | Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia is the third studio album by American rock band The Dandy Warhols.  It was released on August 1, 2000, through record label Capitol.
Godless (song) | "Godless" is a song by American rock band The Dandy Warhols.  It is the third single from their third studio album, "Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia", released on 17 July 2001.

Get Off (The Dandy Warhols song) | "Get Off" is a song by American rock band The Dandy Warhols.  It was released in 2000 as the first single from their third studio album, "Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia", and was re-released in 2002.

Bohemian Like You | "Bohemian Like You" is a song by American alternative rock band The Dandy Warhols.  The song was written by frontman Courtney Taylor-Taylor after seeing a woman pull up in her car to the traffic lights outside his apartment.  It was released as the second single from the band's third studio album, "Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia", in August 2000.

Tales from Eternal Dusk | Tales from Eternal Dusk is the first full album of the German melodic black metal band Dark Fortress.  This was the band's debut album and was released on October 1, 2001.  Though the album was released in 2001, the vinyl version of this album was released four years later in 2005.

Tales from the City | Tales from the City is the second and final studio album by Canadian alternative rock band Mobile.  It was released on October 7, 2008, in Canada.  The debut single from the album, "The Killer", was released on July 1, 2008.

The Sugarman 3 | The Sugarman 3, sometimes titled The Sugarman Three, is a retro-funk band from New York City formed in 1996 by saxophonist Neal Sugarman, Hammond organ player Adam Scone, and drummer Rudy Albin.  The band has released four studio albums—"Sugar's Boogaloo" (1999), "Soul Donkey" (2000), "Pure Cane Sugar" (2002) and "What the World Needs Now" (2012)—and one compilation album, "Sweet Spot" (2001).
response.summary_1:
Evidence found:
- Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia — [1]: "Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia is the third studio album"
- Album release date — [1]: "It was released on August 1, 2000,"
- Single release date — [2]: "released on 17 July 2001."
- Band name (rock) — [1]: "American rock band The Dandy Warhols."
- Band name (alternative rock) — [4]: "\"Bohemian Like You\" is a song by American alternative rock band The Dandy Warhols."
- Song "Godless" — [2]: "\"Godless\" is a song by American rock band The Dandy Warhols."

Ambiguities:
- Album year vs. user phrasing: passage [1] gives album release "August 1, 2000," but passage [2] shows a single released "17 July 2001." The user asked about the "2001 album" which conflicts with passage [1].
- Band label variants: passages refer to The Dandy Warhols as "American rock band" and "American alternative rock band."

Bridge / missing fact:
- The passages do not state the city and state where The Dandy Warhols were formed (formation city, formation state).

Retrieval direction:
1. Open the page "The Dandy Warhols" (band main article).
2. Search query: "The Dandy Warhols formed in" or exact query '"The Dandy Warhols" formed in Portland Oregon'.
3. If needed, open "Thirteen Tales from Urban Bohemia" page for any band-formation notes.
Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets above.
hop2_docs: The Dandy Warhols | The Dandy Warhols are an American alternative rock band, formed in Portland, Oregon in 1994 by singer-guitarist Courtney Taylor-Taylor and guitarist Peter Holmström.  They were joined by keyboardist Zia McCabe and drummer Eric Hedford.  Hedford left in 1998 and was replaced by Taylor-Taylor's cousin Brent DeBoer.  The band's name is a play on the name of American pop artist Andy Warhol.

...The Dandy Warhols Come Down | ...The Dandy Warhols Come Down is the second studio album by American rock band The Dandy Warhols.  It was recorded in 1996 and released on July 15, 1997 by record label Capitol.

...Earth to the Dandy Warhols... | ...Earth to the Dandy Warhols... is the sixth studio album by American alternative rock band The Dandy Warhols, recorded in 2008 and released on May 19, 2008 and is the first album released on their self-founded Beat the World Records label, after leaving Capitol Records in 2007.

The Dandy Warhols Are Sound | The Dandy Warhols Are Sound is a studio album by American alternative rock band The Dandy Warhols.  It is the original mix of the band's 2003 album "Welcome to the Monkey House", by Grammy Award-winning soul music mixing engineer Russell Elevado.  It was released on July 14, 2009, through the band's own label Beat the World Records.

The Black Album/Come On Feel The Dandy Warhols | The Black Album/Come On Feel The Dandy Warhols is a 2004 double album released by American alternative band, The Dandy Warhols.  The two-disc set was released on their own Beat the World Records label.  Initially, the album was only available to purchase through the band's website.  It is now available to own via other sources.

The Dandy Warhols discography | The discography of The Dandy Warhols, an American alternative rock band from Portland, Oregon, consists of nine studio albums, two compilation albums, six extended plays and twenty-eight singles.

Not If You Were the Last Junkie on Earth | "Not If You Were the Last Junkie on Earth" is a song by American rock band The Dandy Warhols.  It was released in 1997 as the second single from their second studio album, "...The Dandy Warhols Come Down".
hop2_titles: The Black Album/Come On Feel The Dandy Warhols
Not If You Were the Last Junkie on Earth
response.summary_2: The Dandy Warhols were formed in Portland, Oregon.
